incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-41940, a critical authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM web hosting control panel software.
incidents.impact: Attackers are gaining unauthorized access to web hosting control panels globally; CISA added to KEV with federal patch deadline of May 3, 2026. Australian Cyber Security Centre confirmed active exploitation affecting Australian businesses.

incidents.victim: US and Ukrainian oil and gas facilities
incidents.vector: Cyberattacks targeting industrial control systems at oil and gas facilities, conducted by Russian hacker Artem Revenskii ("Digit") and co-conspirators.
incidents.impact: Revenskii pleaded guilty in US federal court to attacking US and Ukrainian energy infrastructure. Attacks caused operational disruptions at targeted facilities.

incidents.victim: Multiple SaaS organizations (financial services, retail, healthcare)
incidents.vector: Adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing combined with vishing calls to harvest MFA codes and gain persistent SaaS access, active since October 2025.
incidents.impact: Campaign has compromised SaaS environments across multiple sectors, exfiltrating sensitive data and demanding ransoms. Campaign remains active with ongoing victim targeting.

incidents.victim: ADT — home and business security provider
incidents.vector: Vishing attack against an ADT employee led to compromise of Okta SSO credentials and unauthorized access to internal systems.
incidents.impact: Approximately 5.5 million customer records exfiltrated, including names, addresses, and account details. ADT is notifying affected customers.

incidents.victim: Scattered Spider — criminal group (law enforcement action)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware and data extortion
incidents.vector: US federal prosecution of Tyler Buchanan (UK national), a core Scattered Spider member responsible for SIM swapping, vishing, and ransomware operations against major US companies.
incidents.impact: Buchanan entered a guilty plea to wire fraud charges; sentencing pending. Campaign caused hundreds of millions in losses across victims including MGM Resorts and Caesars Entertainment.

incidents.vector: Two US cybersecurity professionals convicted of operating as ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware affiliates, conducting attacks against US businesses.
incidents.impact: Ryan Goldberg and Kevin Martin each sentenced to 4 years federal prison; co-conspirator Angelo Martino entered a guilty plea. Represents a rare successful prosecution of ransomware actors who were former incident responders.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company / Delta Dental of New York
incidents.vector: Exploitation of MOVEit Transfer zero-day vulnerability (June 2023) to exfiltrate files from DDIC and DDNY MOVEit servers.
incidents.impact: Consumer PII including names, SSNs, driver's license numbers, financial account information, and health data was exfiltrated. NYDFS imposed a $2.25 million penalty for inadequate incident response and failure to timely report the breach.

incidents.victim: SAP ecosystem — npm package users (@cap-js/sqlite, @cap-js/postgres, @cap-js/db-service, mbt)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP threat actor injected malicious code into widely-used SAP npm packages ("Mini Shai-Hulud" campaign), compromising the software supply chain to enable credential theft and data exfiltration.
incidents.impact: Any organization using affected SAP npm packages may have had credentials and sensitive data exfiltrated. Campaign has entered a monetization phase after initial compromise of the supply chain.

incidents.victim: US COVID-19 research institutions; US government Exchange servers (Hafnium campaign)
incidents.vector: Chinese national Xu Zewei, acting on behalf of MSS Shanghai, hacked COVID research organizations and exploited Hafnium vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange servers to steal sensitive research and government communications.
incidents.impact: Xu Zewei extradited from Italy to face US federal charges. Stolen data included COVID-19 research findings and sensitive government email communications, part of a broader MSS-directed cyber espionage campaign.

incidents.vector: Hacking group breached CNAM's national healthcare database, potentially destroying or exfiltrating up to 30% of records containing personal and financial data on Moldovan citizens.
incidents.impact: Personal and financial data of nearly every adult Moldovan who paid national health insurance was exposed. No ransom demanded; Russia's involvement not excluded by Moldovan officials. Incident occurred amid ongoing Russian military threats to Moldova.

incidents.victim: Organizations running Cisco Firepower network security appliances
incidents.vector: Threat actor UAT-4356 deployed FIRESTARTER backdoor malware on Cisco Firepower appliances, exploiting a vulnerability to achieve persistent access on network security devices.
incidents.impact: Affected organizations have persistent adversary presence on network security appliances, enabling long-term surveillance and lateral movement. CISA issued an emergency directive; Cisco Talos and NCSC published joint analysis.

incidents.victim: Western critical infrastructure and government networks
incidents.vector: China-nexus threat actors are operating large-scale covert networks of compromised SOHO routers and IoT devices to disguise the origin of espionage operations, reconnaissance, malware delivery, and data exfiltration.
incidents.impact: A 16-nation joint advisory (NSA, CISA, FBI, Five Eyes, and allied partners) warns of a major shift to botnet-based operational infrastructure by the majority of China-nexus threat actors, making attribution and defense significantly harder.

incidents.victim: Enterprise Microsoft 365 and cloud platform users globally
incidents.vector: Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service platform offered MFA bypass kits to criminal affiliates via QR code lures; Microsoft DCU and Europol executed a coordinated platform takedown in March 2026.
incidents.impact: Platform disrupted after enabling widespread MFA bypass attacks; QR code phishing surged 146% during the period. Microsoft blocked 8.3 billion phishing threats in Q1 2026. Affiliate ecosystem may reconstitute under new infrastructure.

incidents.victim: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google — US frontier AI companies
incidents.vector: Chinese AI labs conducted industrial-scale model distillation attacks using tens of thousands of fraudulent accounts and jailbreaking techniques to extract proprietary capabilities from US frontier AI models.
incidents.impact: Anthropic reported 16 million exchanges extracted via 24,000 fraudulent accounts; Google cited 100,000 queries to Gemini. White House acknowledged the attacks and issued a memo; analysis suggests countermeasures will be insufficient without stronger semiconductor export controls.
